articles.title: Burnham to promise 'more breathing room' for people in first speech as PM
articles.summary: The Labour leader will say he is "acutely conscious" of the UK's high turnover of prime ministers, as he becomes the seventh in 10 years on Monday.

articles.title: Thames Water lenders preparing legal challenge in event of Burnham nationalisation
articles.summary: The BBC understands creditors would would pursue payment in full of the outstanding debts, in the multi-billions of pounds.

articles.title: Russia launches major ballistic missile attack on Ukrainian cities
articles.summary: Eight people die and dozens are injured as several cities are hit by missile and drone attacks, while Kyiv continues its own assault on Russian infrastructure.

articles.title: Tate brothers arrested in US as further UK charges take total to 59
articles.summary: The Crown Prosecution Service says it has requested their extradition from the US, while their lawyer says they are innocent.

articles.title: US soldier killed and one injured after Iranian attack in Iraq
articles.summary: The US military reports another death a day after two of its soldiers were killed in Jordan.

articles.title: Record numbers of honours for working class heroes
articles.summary: Record numbers of people from poorer backgrounds are now receiving awards.

articles.title: The 1975's Matty Healy marries model Gabbriette Bechtel in Los Angeles
articles.summary: The newlyweds were first linked in 2023 after Healy's brief relationship with Taylor Swift.

articles.title: Neighbours actor Terence Donovan dies aged 90
articles.summary: His son, actor Jason Donovan, posted to social media that his father died on Saturday night peacefully in Melbourne, Australia

articles.title: Chinese firm seeks compensation over British Steel nationalisation
articles.summary: Jingye said it will take action "through legal means to the very end" after the UK government nationalised British Steel.

articles.title: Bruno Mars has an unexpected opening before Wembley party starts
articles.summary: After an eight-year absence from UK stages, Bruno Mars returned to Wembley Stadium with a polished, hit-filled show.

articles.title: Fire crews battling blaze on Arthur’s Seat in Edinburgh
articles.summary: A huge plume of smoke could be seen from as far away as Fife on Sunday evening.

articles.title: Rate the Spain and Argentina players in World Cup final
articles.full_text: After the opportunity to rate players has closed, the score displayed represents the average from all the submissions by BBC Sport users.
articles.summary: Rate the Spain and Argentina players out of 10 below and come back 30 minutes after full-time to see the final ratings.

articles.title: Madonna, Justin Bieber, Shakira and BTS perform at World Cup half-time show
articles.summary: Shakira, Robbie Wiliams and Jennifer Hudson were also part of the star-studded final, as Argentina took on Spain for the title.

articles.title: Messi on the brink of history - will it be his last World Cup game?
articles.summary: Argentina captain Lionel Messi will play his 34th World Cup match in Sunday's final, but will it be his last appearance?

articles.title: I've been a World Cup official - I promise you there is no favouritism
articles.summary: Darren Cann speaks to BBC Sport about the conspiracy theories around the World Cup and what referees really think.

articles.title: Trump presence means tight security for World Cup final
articles.summary: Supporters faced long queues to get into New York New Jersey Stadium amid heightened security for the 2026 World Cup final.

articles.title: The hidden cost of the night shift and how to sleep it off
articles.summary: More than three million people in the UK work night shifts, which can greatly impact their health. What can be done to help them?

articles.title: Private jets flock to Montana - but locals can't afford the trailer park
articles.summary: Rents in Bozeman, Montana have skyrocketed as wealthy out-of-towners flock to the city to experience the American West.

articles.title: Can Burnham's casual style survive contact with No 10?
articles.summary: When Burnham becomes PM, he'll find it harder to wear the wardrobe that's central to his political brand.

articles.title: Russians turn to cash, putting more strain on slowing wartime economy
articles.summary: Russians have been hit by mobile internet shutdowns and more businesses are seeking to dodge tax after more than four years of war with Ukraine.

articles.title: Could there be a new dawn for North Sea oil and gas?
articles.summary: The incoming PM is expected to announce fresh support for the North Sea from day one of his premiership.

articles.title: New EU border system tripling time at passport control, airport boss says
articles.summary: Ryanair has also warned passengers travelling to Europe this summer to prepare for extended waits.

articles.title: Marathon runners compete on drifting sea ice at North Pole
articles.summary: Organisers say 23 runners from seven countries took part in races at the Arctic's geographic North Pole.

articles.title: Digital ID plan to be scrapped in Burnham's first major policy pledge
articles.summary: A spokesperson for the incoming prime minister says his government would “put its focus where people need it right now”.

articles.title: Britain 'set for bumper year' for butterflies
articles.summary: A hot and dry spring means a greater variety and more butterflies across the UK, a charity says.

articles.title: Minnesota senior wins law change so she can enjoy Happy Hour at care home
articles.summary: Anita Le Brun, the face of a campaign to allow for alcohol to be served at care homes without a licence, is celebrating its success.

articles.title: How Fox kept cool at baked Birkdale to win The Open
articles.summary: Ryan Fox wins the Open Championship - his first major title - with his final putt on a record-breaking weekend at Royal Birkdale.

articles.title: England seal series with record Lord's ODI total
articles.summary: England hit the highest one-day international total at Lord's to beat India by 27 runs and seal a thrilling 2-1 series victory.

articles.title: Russell 'numb to disappointment' after bitter blow to title hopes
articles.summary: George Russell crashing out at Spa and falling 50 points behind team-mate Kimi Antonelli is a bitter blow to the Briton, writes Andrew Benson.

articles.title: Trump presence means tight security for World Cup final
articles.summary: Supporters faced long queues to get into New York New Jersey Stadium amid heightened security for the 2026 World Cup final.

articles.title: Two riders die in separate British eventing competitions
articles.summary: Two riders have died after separate incidents in competitions on Saturday, British Eventing confirms.

articles.title: Runs and records galore as England beat India to seal ODI series win
articles.summary: Ben Duckett hits a record-breaking 141 to help England beat India by 27 runs in a thrilling series decider at Lord's, with the victory also securing a 2-1 series win.

articles.title: Has Saka shown Tuchel he's too good to leave out?
articles.summary: Bukayo Saka’s World Cup hat-trick against France reignited the selection debate and Thomas Tuchel’s decision to leave him out of the semi-final.
